Ad Text:
Senior AI Consultant - Sydney

💰 $170,000 + Super | Permanent Full-Time | Financial Services

Role Summary

We are seeking a Senior AI Consultant to operate across the full engagement lifecycle - from diagnosing where AI creates value to overseeing the build and delivery of solutions. You will bring structure to ambiguous problems and technical depth to delivery.

Key Responsibilities

Consulting & Value Creation

Diagnose operational and commercial challenges and translate them into AI investment cases.

Lead engagements end-to-end: scoping, delivery, and change management - with a clear line to operational KPIs.

Present recommendations to executives and stakeholders.

AI Due Diligence & Investment Support

Assess how AI affects competitive dynamics, cost structures, and growth trajectories.

Evaluate AI maturity, disruption risk, and post-acquisition value creation potential.

Technical Delivery

Rapidly prototype solutions to validate hypotheses before committing to full builds.

Stay current on emerging AI technologies and identify what genuinely moves the needle.

Qualifications, Skills & Experience

3-7 years in consulting, strategy, or technical problem-solving - ideally with exposure to PE-backed businesses.

Proven ability to run structured engagements: framing, stakeholder management, and on-time delivery.

Strong understanding of AI - able to separate signal from hype and provide practical guidance.

Hands-on experience with AI deployments and technical delivery.

Clear, confident communicator - comfortable in both technical deep-dives and executive briefings.

Exposure to frameworks and methodologies common in Big4 advisory or similar environments is highly regarded.

📩 Apply Now
Bring your AI expertise and strategic insight to a senior consulting role - helping shape AI-driven transformation in a dynamic, innovative environment.

The Company

SpinTel is one of Australia's leading independent full-service telecommunications providers. Established in 1996, SpinTel continues to deliver high-quality services and unrivalled customer support. Recently, SpinTel has substantially increased its already impressive customer base – an impressive achievement for a business that is 100 per cent Australian-owned.

As we continue innovating and expanding our capabilities, we’re building an AI team that thrives on curiosity, collaboration and real-world impact. If you’re excited about shaping the future of AI at a company that values both technology and people, you’ll fit right in here.

 

The Role

We’re looking for an AI Prompt Engineer to join our team and help shape the future of data-driven analysis/Chat within the company.

This role will focus on designing and developing AI-driven analytical solutions to enhance our quality assurance processes and chatbots, thereby improving service standards, operational efficiency, and customer experience. You will work closely with analysts, technical teams, and business stakeholders to develop AI tools that transform data into actionable insights and support smarter decision-making across the organisation.

You will also play a key role in designing and optimising prompts and AI workflows that enable large language models (LLMs) and machine learning systems to perform effectively within business processes.

What can we offer you?

Convenient location in comfortable modern offices in Sydney CBD

Able to work from home after six months.

Close to transport.

Great Friendly Team Environment.

Four-day work week.

 

We are looking for someone who has:

Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Computer Science, Data Science, Statistics, Mathematics, Artificial Intelligence, Machine Learning, or a related field

Strong collaboration and communication skills, with the ability to work effectively with analysts and business stakeholders

Industry experience in Data Science, Machine Learning, or AI, with a proven track record of developing and deploying analytical models

Experience applying AI or machine learning techniques to real-world data analysis problems

Strong analytical and problem-solving skills, with the ability to translate complex datasets into meaningful insights

Experience working with large datasets and data analysis tools

Proficiency in Python and SQL

Familiarity with AI and machine learning frameworks such as OpenAI APIs

Experience designing, testing and optimising AI prompts or AI-driven workflows to support business processes

Experience working with code repositories such as GitHub

 

Desirable

Bilingual communication skills

Understanding of customer-focused and business-driven Quality Assurance frameworks

Familiarity with the telecommunications industry and operational processes

Knowledge of large language models (LLMs), generative AI applications, and prompt engineering techniques

Job Title: Lead Full Stack Engineer - AI-Enabled Platforms
Ad Text:
Lead Full Stack Engineer - AI-Enabled Platforms
 
Python | FastAPI | React | TypeScript | AWS
 
We're partnering with a large enterprise building the next generation of AI-enabled digital platforms.

This role is for a Lead Full Stack Engineer who enjoys owning systems end-to-end — from modern frontend product experiences through to high-performance backend services and APIs.
 
You'll lead the development of production-grade applications that embed AI capabilities into real software products, with a strong emphasis on Python backend engineering, FastAPI services and MCP-enabled integrations.
 
This is a hands-on technical leadership role for an engineer who enjoys designing and building scalable systems that ship to users, not just prototypes or research environments.
 
What You'll Build
You'll help design and deliver scalable applications that:

Power enterprise AI-enabled workflows and decision platforms
Deliver modern React-based product experiences
Expose scalable Python / FastAPI backend services and APIs
Integrate LLM capabilities through MCP-enabled service layers
Operate reliably at enterprise scale with strong observability and performance
Your work will span frontend, backend and platform layers, collaborating closely with product, platform and engineering teams.
 
What You'll Do

Design and build modern full stack applications used across the enterprise
Develop Python backend services using FastAPI to power AI-enabled products
Build and maintain high-performance APIs and service layers supporting enterprise workloads
Integrate LLM-powered capabilities through Model Context Protocol (MCP) frameworks
Develop React + TypeScript interfaces supporting complex enterprise workflows
Architect data flows, APIs and services that support AI-powered product features
Deliver reliable software through CI/CD pipelines, automated testing and strong observability practices
Lead engineering decisions around system architecture, scalability and service design
Mentor engineers and contribute to engineering standards and best practices
 
What You Bring

6+ years experience building production software as a full stack engineer
Strong backend engineering capability in Python
Experience building production services with FastAPI or similar async Python frameworks
Strong Python engineering capability is the most important requirement for this role — experience with LLM frameworks, MCP or AI integrations can be developed on the job.

Experience designing scalable APIs, microservices and service-oriented architectures
Experience integrating AI capabilities into production software products
Experience with Model Context Protocol (MCP) or similar AI integration frameworks
Strong experience with React and TypeScript
Cloud-native development experience on AWS
Ability to lead technical decisions while remaining hands-on with code
 
Core Engineering Stack

Backend: Python, FastAPI, MCP frameworks
Frontend: React, TypeScript
AI Systems: LLM integrations, RAG pipelines, vector databases
Data Platform: Databricks Lakehouse
Cloud: AWS
Platform: CI/CD pipelines, automated testing, observability tooling
Architecture: APIs, microservices, event-driven systems
 
Why This Role

Build real software products used across the enterprise
Work across frontend, backend and AI-enabled service layers
Lead development of Python-based application platforms
Integrate modern AI capabilities into production systems
Influence the technical direction of enterprise-scale platforms
 
If you're a strong Python engineer who enjoys building scalable backend systems while delivering modern full stack applications, this is a rare opportunity to help shape the next generation of AI-enabled enterprise platforms.
 
Apply now or reach out for a confidential discussion.

Job Title: Senior Data Scientist - Engineering & Architecture Group
Ad Text:
Overview

Do you enjoy solving problems, looking at problems through a different lens, and working closely with customers to innovate new solutions to complex problems? Do you jump with excitement at the opportunity to identify trends and provide unique business solutions? Do you want to join a team where learning about a new technology or solution is part of our work every day? 

The Industry Solutions Delivery (ISD) Engineering & Architecture Group (EAG) is a global consulting and engineering organization that supports our most complex and leading-edge customer engagements.  Driving early-stage deliveries, enhances ISD’s technical capabilities, and partnering with others to develop approaches, innovative solutions, and engineering standards in order to set our sales and delivery teams up for success.  We provide consistent high-quality customer experience through technical and AI leadership and IP capture centred on delivery truth. 

Responsibilities

Engage stakeholders to understand business drivers and confidently communicate insights, recommendations, and tradeoffs 

Partner with customers to translate complex business problems into data science solutions with clear objectives, assumptions, risks, and success criteria 

Work closely with Solution Architects, Consultants, and Data Engineers who will source and prepare data from multiple systems. 

Explore and evaluate data quality, integrity, and fitness for purpose, clearly explaining implications to customers.  

Design and deliver machine learning solutions covering Generative AI, Agentic solutions, computer vision, speech processing,  forecasting, NLP, classification, clustering, reinforcement learning and optimization.  

Frame the problem appropriately; and select and apply appropriate algorithms, modelling techniques, and experimental methods to meet client and project requirements  

Conduct statistical analysis and controlled experiments, translating findings into measurable business outcomes  

Support model testing and validation using test and production data, incorporating customer feedback into improvements  

Collaborate with engineering teams to deploy scalable, secure, and reliable models into customer environments  

Manage customer expectations on scope, timelines, and outcomes, contributing to strong delivery experiences  

Stay current with industry trends and contribute to thought leadership and reusable intellectual property 

Qualifications

Required/Minimum Qualifications 

Master’s degree in Data Science, Mathematics, Statistics, Econometrics, Economics, Operations Research, Computer Science, or a related field and 7+ years of professional data science experience 
or 

Doctorate in Data Science, Mathematics, Statistics, Econometrics, Economics, Operations Research, Computer Science, or a related field and 4+ years of professional data science experience 
or 

Equivalent practical experience demonstrated through long-term, hands-on delivery of data science solutions 

Strong foundation in data science principles, including statistics, machine learning, and analytical modelling 

Proven problem-solving skills with the ability to break down complex business and technical challenges 

Handson experience developing and deploying machine learning and advanced AI solutions to production environments on public cloud platforms 

Professional experience working with Generative AI solutions in production environments (beyond pilots or POC /proof of concepts) 

This position will be open for a minimum of 5 days, with applications accepted on an ongoing basis until the position is filled.

Microsoft is an equal opportunity employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to age, ancestry, citizenship, color, family or medical care leave, gender identity or expression, genetic information, immigration status, marital status, medical condition, national origin, physical or mental disability, political affiliation, protected veteran or military status, race, ethnicity, religion, sex (including pregnancy), sexual orientation, or any other characteristic protected by applicable local laws, regulations and ordinances. If you need assistance with religious accommodations and/or a reasonable accommodation due to a disability during the application process, read more about requesting accommodations.

Overview


Why This Role 

At Microsoft, you’ll work at the forefront of cloud and AI innovation, helping customers turn cutting edge technology into real, production ready solutions. You’ll have access to worldclass platforms, deep technical communities, and the opportunity to work on meaningful, largescale customer challenges across industries. 

You’ll join a group of high calibre, genuinely collaborative architects who care deeply about doing the right thing for customers. This is a team that: 

Shares knowledge freely and lifts each other up 

Balances technical depth with practical delivery 

Values ownership, trust, and strong engineering fundamentals 

Encourages experimentation, learning, and continuous improvement 

You won’t be expected to “figure it out alone.” You’ll be surrounded by people who are smart, supportive, and passionate about building great AI solutions, while still having the autonomy to shape how you work and where you focus. 

If you’re excited about responsible AI, modern application architectures, and making a real impact with customers, this is a role where you can do some of the best work of your career. 

About the Role 

We’re looking for a Cloud Solution Architect (AI Specialist) to help customers design, build, and operationalize AI powered solutions on Azure. 
You’ll work directly with enterprise customers as a trusted technical advisor, guiding them from early AI experimentation through secure, scalable, production ready AI solutions. 

This role blends deep technical expertise, hands on architecture, and customer engagement, with a strong focus on Agentic AI / autonomous agents, and modern application architectures. 

 Responsibilities

Partner with customers to architect and deliver AI enabled solutions on Azure, from design through deployment and operational readiness. 

Translate business goals and technical requirements into secure, scalable AI and application architectures. 

Lead technical workshops, architecture reviews, and solution deep dives with customer and account teams. 

Help customers move from POCs and pilots into production, with a strong focus on reliability, security, cost, and responsible AI. 

Collaborate across engineering, security, and delivery teams to drive adoption, usage, and measurable business outcomes. 

Qualifications

Required Experience 

4+ years experience in cloud, software engineering, solution architecture, or technical consulting roles. 

Working knowledge of Azure application, data, and AI services, and identity and security fundamentals. 

Experience using AI assisted developer tools such as GitHub Copilot to improve developer productivity and code quality. 

Experience translating customer requirements into technical designs, validating assumptions, and delivering solutions aligned to best practices. 

Handson experience improving delivery quality through infrastructure a scode, CI/CD optimisation, test automation, or pipeline modernisation. 

Experience working directly with customers or internal stakeholders in a technical advisory capacity, including architecture discussions and solution reviews. 

Ability to communicate clearly with engineering teams, architects, and nontechnical stakeholders, adapting depth and language to the audience. 

Demonstrated independence, ownership, and rapid rampup in new technical or customer environments. 

Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Engineering, or equivalent practical experience. 

Preferred Experience 

6+ years experience delivering complex, customer facing technical solutions. 

Experience delivering production grade AI or modern application workloads. 

Strong understanding of AI models, agents, and orchestration patterns, across Microsoft and partner ecosystems. 

Experience creating, integrating with, or operating agentic orchestration and/or MCP (Model Context Protocol) Servers. 

Experience with enterprise or hybrid cloud architectures, including migrations, modernization, or platform governance. 

Proficiency in .NET, Java, JavaScript/Node.js, or Python, applied in production systems. 

Experience with PaaS and Kubernetes (e.g. AKS) and CaaS platforms (e.g. ACA) and FaaS (e.g. Azure Functions). 

Experience influencing senior level technical or business stakeholders and guiding architectural decisions. 

Exposure to government or regulated enterprise environments, with awareness of security, privacy, and compliance considerations. 

Cloud, AI, or security certifications (Azure or equivalent). 

What Makes You Successful 

You’re comfortable moving between deep technical detail and executive level conversation. 

You take ownership and have a strong bias for action and delivery. 

You care about real customer outcomes, not just demos or proofs of concept. 

You bring a pragmatic, “get it done” mindset while maintaining strong engineering and security standards. 

You’re excited about helping customers adopt AI responsibly and at scale. 

This position will be open for a minimum of 5 days, with applications accepted on an ongoing basis until the position is filled.

Microsoft is an equal opportunity employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to age, ancestry, citizenship, color, family or medical care leave, gender identity or expression, genetic information, immigration status, marital status, medical condition, national origin, physical or mental disability, political affiliation, protected veteran or military status, race, ethnicity, religion, sex (including pregnancy), sexual orientation, or any other characteristic protected by applicable local laws, regulations and ordinances. Company Description


Join the team redefining how the world experiences design.

Hey, g'day, mabuhay, kia ora, 你好, hallo, vítejte!

Thanks for stopping by. We know job hunting can be a little time consuming and you're probably keen to find out what's on offer, so we'll get straight to the point.

Where and how you can work

Our flagship campus is in Sydney. We also have a campus in Melbourne and co-working spaces in Brisbane, Perth and Adelaide. But you have choice in where and how you work, we trust our Canvanauts to choose the balance that empowers them and their team to achieve their goals.

What you’d be doing in this role

As Canva scales change continues to be part of our DNA. But we like to think that's all part of the fun. So this will give you the flavour of the type of things you'll be working on when you start, but this will likely evolve.

Job Description


About the team

Join the innovation powerhouse - Content Machine Learning team at Canva! Our vision is to empower all our users with the safest and highest quality content at scale. We build models and human-in-the-loop workflows to amplify our content review efforts so that we can:

Unlock review operations with review automation

Close the loop for feedback to our models and raise the quality bar

Ensure the safety of our library for all our users, including students

Finally, our crazy big goal is to build an automated review system with humans at the heart of operations, leading the continuous improvement and evolution of the system.

As the Engineering Manager for Content ML, you'll coach a team of talented and high performing bunch of machine learning engineers working across content moderation, enrichment, review, and scalable automation. You'll define the team’s vision and guide strategic delivery aligned with Canva's goals. You’ll collaborate closely with content experts, review operations, product managers, and machine learning teams to create infrastructure that makes millions of assets reviewed every day.

About the team

Join the innovation powerhouse - Content Machine Learning team at Canva! Our vision is to empower all our users with the safest and highest quality content at scale. We build models and human-in-the-loop workflows to amplify our content review efforts so that we can:

Unlock review operations with review automation

Close the loop for feedback to our models and raise the quality bar

Ensure the safety of our library for all our users, including students

Finally, our crazy big goal is to build an automated review system with humans at the heart of operations, leading the continuous improvement and evolution of the system.

As the Engineering Manager for Content ML, you'll coach a team of talented and high performing bunch of machine learning engineers working across content moderation, enrichment, review, and scalable automation. You'll define the team’s vision and guide strategic delivery aligned with Canva's goals. You’ll collaborate closely with content experts, review operations, product managers, and machine learning teams to create infrastructure that makes millions of assets reviewed every day.

What you’ll do

Lead and grow a high-performing team of Machine Learning Engineers focused on automating content review and moderation across Canva’s Content Library.

Set and execute the technical vision and strategy for ML-driven safety, quality, and policy enforcement across text, image, video, audio and template content.

Design and scale robust ML systems — including experimentation frameworks, evaluation pipelines, monitoring, and feedback loops to continuously improve model performance and close the gap between manual review and automation.

Partner cross-functionally with Content, Product, Review Ops, and Review Engineering to deliver impactful, user-facing solutions and scalable human-in-the-loop systems.

Drive large, end-to-end initiatives from problem framing and roadmap definition through to delivery, experimentation, rollout, and impact measurement.

Uplift engineering excellence through strong technical direction, pragmatic architecture decisions, and a healthy code review and knowledge-sharing culture.

Coach and mentor engineers to navigate complex technical challenges and do the best work of their lives.

Champion operational excellence, responsible AI, and safety-by-design, ensuring our systems serve all users, including students and educators, with quality, integrity, and trust.

Stay ahead of industry trends and emerging ML best practices, continuously evolving the domain to meet Canva’s scale and ambition.

You're probably a match if

You’re passionate about coaching and developing engineers, creating a culture of growth and support.

You’re experienced in designing systems at scale, with a focus on reliability, performance, and extensibility.

You collaborate cross-functionally and thrive in partnerships with product, ML, and content stakeholders.

You think strategically and deliver pragmatically, balancing long-term vision with immediate needs.

You value diversity, empathy, and inclusion, and lead in a way that fosters belonging and trust.

You have experience leading an engineering team in a Product or SaaS environment, ideally within ML or applied AI domains.

Prior experience in text, image, video, and audio understanding is highly desirable.

Additional Information


What's in it for you?

Achieving our crazy big goals motivates us to work hard - and we do - but you'll experience lots of moments of magic, connectivity and fun woven throughout life at Canva, too. We also offer a range of benefits to set you up for every success in and outside of work.

Here's a taste of what's on offer:

Equity packages - we want our success to be yours too

Inclusive parental leave policy that supports all parents & carers

An annual Vibe & Thrive allowance to support your wellbeing, social connection, office setup & more

Flexible leave options that empower you to be a force for good, take time to recharge and supports you personally

Check out lifeatcanva.com for more info.

Other stuff to know

We make hiring decisions based on your experience, skills and passion, as well as how you can enhance Canva and our culture. When you apply, please tell us the pronouns you use and any reasonable adjustments you may need during the interview process.

We celebrate all types of skills and backgrounds at Canva so even if you don’t feel like your skills quite match what’s listed above - we still want to hear from you!

Please note that interviews are conducted virtually.

* Are you a senior automation engineer who thrives on designing complex AMR and warehouse automation solutions end-to-end?
* Do you enjoy owning major projects from pre-sales discovery through to go-live and optimisation?
* Are you ready to be the technical authority delivering large-scale robotics deployments across VIC and ANZ?

Our Client:
A global tier 1 automation and intralogistics leader is seeking a customer-focused, technically strong and commercially astute Senior Solutions Engineer to join their growing VIC team.

With recent growth and project wins - including a $35M warehouse automation deployment in Melbourne - the business is investing heavily in Autonomous Mobile Robotics (AMR) and integrated distribution centre solutions across ANZ.

In this role, you will take full ownership of complex AMR projects from early-stage discovery through to implementation, go-live and optimisation. This is not purely a design role - it is end-to-end technical leadership across live operational environments.

Key Responsibilities:
* Lead technical consulting and solution design for complex AMR and warehouse automation deployments.
* Own projects end-to-end - from pre-sales scoping, discovery and solution modelling through to implementation and optimisation.
* Act as the senior technical authority bridging commercial strategy, engineering and delivery execution.
* Serve as the primary client technical contact throughout implementation and deployment phases.
* Conduct operational discovery including site visits, workshops, future-state process design and assumptions modelling.
* Translate warehouse operational data into engineered, investment-ready automation solutions.
* Design integrated automated and manual DC workflows and system architectures.
* Develop AutoCAD layouts, system sizing, cost estimates, timelines and deployment strategies.
* Ensure OH&S and compliance standards are upheld across all deployments.
* Mentor and guide engineering team members across design, testing and documentation.
* Drive continuous improvement initiatives across projects and post-delivery support models.

About You:
You are a confident, client-facing senior solutions professional with strong operational insight and commercial judgement. You understand live warehouse environments and how automation integrates into real-world DC operations.
You enjoy translating complexity into practical engineered solutions and take accountability beyond concept design through to successful delivery.

You Bring:
* 5+ years operational experience within warehousing, logistics or distribution environments.
* Strong knowledge of warehouse automation and AMR systems.
* Proven experience designing integrated automated + manual DC operations.
* Advanced AutoCAD skills (essential).
* Strong analytical capability - able to convert operational data into engineered solutions.
* Experience across pre-sales, solution design and project delivery environments.
* A personable, proactive and collaborative mindset with the confidence to influence stakeholders.

This is rewarding opportunity to join a market leader that offers great autonomy, domain leadership and a hybrid work model.

More than a solutions engineering role - it's an opportunity to lead high-impact deployments that transform major warehouse operations across VIC and ANZ, within a fast-growing global automation landscape.

To apply or learn more about this opportunity, contact:
Lee Luu on 0427 005 272 or l.luu@proactiveglobal.com
Rose Mbugua on 0489 902 670 or r.mbugua@proactiveglobal.com

Job Title: Senior Software Engineer-- AI / API Platform
Ad Text:
Senior Software Engineer - AI & API Platforms

We're partnering with a large enterprise organisation undergoing a major AI-driven transformation of their customer platforms. This program focuses on building next-generation AI-enabled user interfaces, modernising legacy systems, and developing scalable API platforms to support intelligent digital services.

This is a greenfield-heavy environment where engineers will help define patterns, break down legacy systems, and build modern services that power AI-driven experiences.

You'll be joining a high-performing agile squad working at the intersection of AI, APIs, and modern cloud architecture.

The Role

As a Senior Software Engineer, you will help design and build the next generation of platform services that power AI-enabled applications.

Key work includes:

Building and evolving API platforms in .NET

Developing services that support AI agents and intelligent user interfaces

Contributing to Next.js front-end platforms that consume backend APIs

Supporting Python-based AI agent services

Migrating legacy monolithic applications into modern service-based architectures

Working across data migration, service domains, and system integrations

Collaborating with distributed teams including offshore engineering teams

Helping establish engineering patterns and best practices for AI-enabled systems

Tech Stack

.NET / C# (core backend API development)

Python (AI agents and services)

Next.js / modern web UI

REST APIs & microservices architecture

Cloud platforms and modern data architectures

Agile delivery environments

What We're Looking For

We're looking for engineers who can hit the ground running in complex enterprise environments.

Key experience:

Strong .NET API development experience

Experience working with modern distributed architectures

Exposure to Python or AI-driven development

Experience building API-first platforms

Comfortable working in fast-moving Agile squads

Ability to navigate large, complex organisations

Strong communication skills and ability to work with technical and business stakeholders

Nice to Have

Experience with AI tools or LLM platforms (e.g. Claude, GPT, etc.)

Exposure to agentic AI systems

Financial services or large enterprise environment experience

Experience working with legacy modernisation or system migrations

Why This Role?

$1,200 per day + super

12-month contract with strong extension potential

Work on cutting-edge AI-driven platforms

Join a high-impact transformation program

Collaborative engineering culture with modern technology stack

We are seeking a Senior Data Scientist to join an Intelligent Solutions Delivery team, supporting the delivery of AI‑enabled solutions for Claims and enterprise transformation initiatives. This role is focused on building, validating, and productionising AI and machine learning solutions that are embedded directly into business workflows, with a strong emphasis on responsible, governed, and scalable AI delivery.

You will work closely with AI Solution Architects, Engineers, Claims SMEs, and Delivery Squads to develop models and intelligent components using Databricks AI/ML frameworks, Python, and SQL, contributing to agent‑based, predictive, and decision‑support solutions across the claims lifecycle.

Key Responsibilities

Design, build, and iterate machine learning and AI solutions that support Claims transformation initiatives (e.g. triage, summarisation, risk detection, decision support).
Develop analytical and ML components that can be embedded into agentic and AI‑assisted workflows.

Build and manage models using Databricks AI/ML frameworks, leveraging scalable compute and governed data access.
Use Python for feature engineering, model training, evaluation, and inference logic.
Use SQL to extract, transform, and validate data used in AI and ML pipelines.
Contribute to reusable notebooks, pipelines, and patterns that accelerate AI delivery across squads.
Support the transition of models from experimentation to production, working with engineers on deployment patterns within Databricks.
Implement model documentation, performance tracking, and validation artefacts to support model risk, compliance, and audit requirements.
Monitor model behaviour over time (performance, drift, stability) and recommend improvements.

Skills & Experience (Required)

Proven experience delivering data science or machine learning solutions in a production or enterprise environment.
Strong hands‑on experience with Python for data analysis and ML development.
Experience working with Databricks, including AI/ML workloads and notebook‑based development.
Solid SQL capability for data exploration, feature creation, and validation.
Experience across the ML lifecycle: problem framing, data preparation, modelling, evaluation, and operational handover.
Strong communication skills and ability to work in cross‑functional delivery teams.
Partner with Intelligent Solutions Delivery squads, Solution Architects, and Engineers to ensure AI components align to end‑to‑end solution design.
Collaborate with Claims, Operations, and Risk stakeholders to validate assumptions and ensure models are interpretable and fit‑for‑purpose.

Desirable Experience

Exposure to agentic AI, GenAI, NLP, or AI‑assisted workflows.
Experience working in regulated or risk‑aware environments (e.g. financial services, insurance).
Familiarity with MLOps practices (model versioning, reproducibility, monitoring).
Understanding of ethical AI, explainability, and governance considerations.

Job Title: Principal Consultant - Azure Data Engineer. Brisbane
Ad Text:

Ready to Lead with Us?

Apply now to join Agile Insights and take your career to the next level!

The Organisation:

MHM Automation (MHM) designs and delivers automated systems for the global food processing and logistics industries. Our solutions enhance efficiency, yield, and worker safety by integrating advanced technologies with innovative engineering.

Founded in Christchurch in 1884, MHM has grown from a regional engineering business into a trusted international provider of automation systems for the food sector. With deep roots in New Zealand’s manufacturing and export industries, we are proud of our 140-year history and global reach across dairy, meat, cheese, fruit, and vegetable processing.

In 2024, MHM became part of Fortifi Food Processing Solutions, supporting Fortifi’s strategic expansion into non-protein food sectors and strengthening its presence across the Asia-Pacific region. Today, MHM operates across five continents through four key business units – Wyma NZ, Wyma EU, Fabrication, and Materials Handling – contributing to the delivery of full-line automated solutions worldwide.


The Role:

Based out of our Brisbane office, you will be using your programming skills to build, install, and commission automated mechanical systems for our Chilling and Freezing projects. You will manage these projects and ensure their success by designing and troubleshooting electrical issues, interpreting electrical drawings, and developing software solutions for our customers. This is an exciting opportunity where you can provide services to our external stakeholders, while being a key driver for individual growth within the organisation. Communication and interpersonal skills are vital as you support your team with fault-finding, technical tasks, and scheduling.

Have a good eye for detail and high level of accuracy


We Can Offer:

A competitive remuneration  

An opportunity to grow your career

The opportunity to be part of a very progressive and successful organisation


MHM Automation has 4 product centres of excellence across Australia and New Zealand.  Despite our diversity and business breadth we are small enough for each person to make a difference through our values – we are bold, we are aspirational, we are in it together, we stand by it.

Define clearly what logic runs:

• on the device firmware
• in the application layer
• in backend services

Ensure the architecture allows for future integrations with additional technologies, devices and services.

Document architecture decisions and technical trade-offs.


Application Development

Improve and stabilise the existing MVP application.

Responsibilities include:

• implementing core interaction logic
• improving system reliability
• supporting tactile, non-visual and voice-assisted interaction patterns
• collaborating closely with product design

The focus is functionality and stability rather than consumer polish.




Hardware ↔ Software Integration

Work with the hardware engineer to define and implement the communication layer between the device and the application, including:

• BLE or similar communication protocols
• command structures
• state handling and synchronisation
• debugging and reliability tools




AI & Voice Integration

Support integration of voice-assisted AI features that allow users to interact with the system through spoken input and audio feedback.

Ensure the architecture can support future AI capabilities and integrations.




Must Have Experience

• Experience working with hardware-connected products
• Experience integrating software with physical devices (BLE, firmware interfaces or similar)
• Experience building companion applications, including:
– native mobile applications
– cross-platform mobile apps
– web applications wrapped as mobile apps

• Strong system architecture and integration skills
• Experience taking prototypes toward production-ready systems




Contractor Requirements

• Must operate as an independent contractor with a valid ABN
• Must be able to invoice for contract work
• Must hold Professional Indemnity Insurance
• Must hold Public Liability Insurance
• Sydney-based preferred for limited in-person collaboration


Deliverables

By the end of the engagement we expect:

• a stable integrated software system connected to the device
• reliable hardware–software communication
• improved stability and usability of the MVP application
• software suitable for manufacturing preparation and pilot testing
• architecture documented to support future integrations and scaling




Hiring Process

Screening call

Interview

Technical interview + paid task (2-3hours)

Start engagement

We are looking for someone available to start immediately.




How to Apply

Please send:

• CV / Resume
• Portfolio or relevant project examples - ideally hardware & software
• GitHub


IP & Legal

All intellectual property created during the engagement remains the property of the company.

A standard NDA and contractor agreement will be provided prior to commencement.
